text:
Long ago, in a country far away, a village lived a humble girl named Melchora. She loved butterflies. Melchora would hang little fluttering nets around her windows to attract them. Her little dog had a big floppy hat that resembled a butterfly's wings.

One day, while Melchora was catching magnificent Monarchs for her nets, she spotted something beautiful. It wasn't a butterfly; it was a great purple butterfly. It fluttered over her hat.

She was captivated.

For years, she watched as that fabulous butterfly emerged once a year to admire Mount Montoya, a huge old cherry tree kissed by sunshine all year round.

When the great purple butterfly never returned the next year, Melchora felt a sad loneliness she didn't understand. She sat on a branch waiting eagerly for the big farewell flutter.

That became her ritual—each time the big butterfly departed, Melchora would sit patiently for weeks.

Finally one spring morning, while Melchora was singing
